summary: Solid soundboard recording that captures a laid‑back late‑80s show with strong moments like Black Muddy River and Sugaree, though the performance feels typical and occasionally low‑energy.
review: The SBD capture is clear and reliable – reviewers repeatedly note good board quality and crisp instrument balance. Musically the band plays well and delivers a few highlights (Black Muddy River, Sugaree, an enjoyable Iko Iko), but many feel the setlist is fairly typical of the era and at times a bit subdued.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the soundboard quality (good‑to‑excellent) while the musical performance is judged solid but unexceptional, yielding an overall rating in the mid‑range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as clear and crisp; “right on” for an SBD. A few listeners call it merely decent, but no major drop‑outs or muffiness are reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical late‑80s Dead set – well‑played and cohesive in spots (notably the “We Want Phil” chant), but overall laid‑back and lacking the high energy of earlier eras.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for strong guitar tone and solos; reviewers say his playing makes them smile.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned positively in moments like the “We Want Phil” chant and noted as interesting when paired with Jerry on certain tracks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s Hammond B‑3 organ work is highlighted as a standout element (“cannot be beat”).
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space are described as sufficient but not especially memorable.
